note: Sprint 5.22b hotfix: scientific intake worker backend. Operator's first real e2e run of Sprints 5.20 -> 5.21 -> 5.22 failed at the worker step with 'unknown source_tool: trinity_scientific_prompt_intake' — the task_builder + schema accepted the new enum value but useful_compute_worker.py and useful_compute_backends.py didn't. This hotfix lands on the same branch trinity/operator-loop-from-existing-request-v01 as Sprint 5.22. Changes: useful_compute_backends.py gains a placeholder_scientific_intake backend (PLACEHOLDER_KIND, task_type=scientific_intake) that produces a deterministic hash-manifest output containing source_tool / task_type / request_id / input_bundle_sha256 / metadata.scientific_intake identifiers + 'validation_status: hash_manifest_only' + a 'no LLM, no network' disclaimer. _TASK_TYPES tuple extended with 'scientific_intake'. useful_compute_worker.py _SOURCE_TOOLS enum now includes 'trinity_scientific_prompt_intake'; _TASK_TYPES tuple gains 'scientific_intake'; allowed top-level request keys now include 'metadata'; when source_tool is the scientific-intake bridge OR task_type is scientific_intake, validate_request requires metadata.scientific_intake with the six identifier/hash/count fields (intake_id matches ^spi-[0-9a-f]{16}$, combined_context_sha256 / prompt_sha256 / intake_artifact_sha256 are 64-hex, documents_count is non-negative int, intake_task_kind enum [benchmark|comparison|extraction|validation]). New tests/trinity/test_scientific_pipeline_e2e.py reproduces the user-reported failure end-to-end and asserts 7 invariants: worker accepts the new source_tool, worker rejects without metadata, worker rejects with bad metadata, two workers produce identical compute_output_sha256 (replay contract holds for the new task_type), backend output carries every intake identifier verbatim, full pipeline completes through payment_draft (unsigned-only / no_broadcast / real_signed=false), two e2e runs produce the same operator_run_id. Trinity suite: 967 passed, 10 skipped. No consensus, no RPC, no wallet, no signer, no wire-format change. No new subprocess. The new backend never calls a network, an LLM, or any external service.
